title: Podman System Reset --run-root --graph-root
body:
added options to reinstantiate storage after resetting the podman system using
podman system reset. --run-root and --graph-root define the runRoot and graphRoot to initiate
a new runtime with. these values, once populated into a new libpd runtime, then are used to create a new storage.conf
file at the proper location (root or rootless default location).

These options allow for a more interactive and useful reset program and ill help eliminate many questions regarding how to configure the basic entities
of container storage.

depends on containers/storage#1096

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

<!--

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: brought in a bunch of stuff when getting the new c/storage release, not sure if I grabbed anything extra or not...
@cdoern  Is this something we need to get into podman 4.0, or can it wait for podman 4.1?
circling back to this tonight, the tests need to be completely rewritten but the features are done. This by no means needs to be 4.0

@rhatdan I think integrations tests might work here. Is there a way to set my storage.conf via an env variable or a global podman option? If so I can make a dummy file and write/read from that within the testing suite

CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF=/PATH/storage.conf podman ...
```
man podman
...
   CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF
       Set default location of the storage.conf file.
```

@rhatdan is there any reason `types.Save` in `libpod/reset.go` removes and does not update the storage.conf? I am testing this locally now and it seems to just remove the file but the function looks right to me.
It should work, I have a feeling, you are not saving what you think you are.
@rhatdan can we cut a new c/storage release so I can fix this up?

You do not need a release.  Just take the current checksum and use that rather then a version.

```
$ git diff go.mod
diff --git a/go.mod b/go.mod
index 79880469b..f6a2e0f1d 100644
--- a/go.mod
+++ b/go.mod
@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@ require (
        github.com/containers/image/v5 v5.19.1
        github.com/containers/ocicrypt v1.1.2
        github.com/containers/psgo v1.7.2
-       github.com/containers/storage v1.38.2
+       github.com/containers/storage 77cf15bc7897a70cd2e0b026ac1fe9757b791075
        github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22 v22.3.2
        github.com/coreos/stream-metadata-go v0.0.0-20210225230131-70edb9eb47b3
        github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin v0.2.3
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@Luap99 it looks like yours did not work because you do not have a storage.conf. We only honor the flags if the file exists int the default dir. I can add a warning for that
Should we support his use case?

@Luap99 I addressed many of your review comments with some questions on some. I guess it is up to you really if you want the use case supported.

I think the reason we opted not to @rhatdan is because then people could pass these options unknowingly and mess up their storage config in a pretty major way when there was none to begin with.

If it doesn't work when there is no storage.conf then there is not much value for this feature. If I already have a storage.conf I can easily manually add the runroot and graphroot.

Friendly remainder that we had pretty bad experiences in the past with writing config files. I am not sure if want want more of such problem with storage.conf.
I tested this and it writes 0 numbers into the config. This can be very bad if the default is not actually 0. We had these problem with containers.conf in the past. You have to fix this. The toml library uses omitzero for numeric values and not omitempty.


Also why does this remove my new graphroot and runroot directories, because of this it is not possible to populate them with existing images for example?
And even more important this will not remove my old graph/runroot so system reset is not functional in this case.
IMO the logic should do a proper reset and only then create the new the graph/runroot. If you do this you can avoid all the db conflicts that your are currently handling in your code.
@Luap99 so your recommendations are 1) fix the toml writing in c/storage and 2) make this write even when a config file does not exist?

Well first I am not sure if this feature is really needed since you could just add the config value manually as user.
But I guess we have some form of request for this otherwise you would not work on this.

1) yes you need to fix this in c/storage
2) I would think that this makes more sense

But more importantly this does not work yet because you are not actually deleting to old location. IMO this must do a full reset and only then create the new storage.conf. I do not see why we need all this tricky special db handling here.

@Luap99 I worked on this because I thought reset was lacking customization. If we think this only introduces more issues I guess I can close this. I was just trying to create more options to make the feature more helpful.

@rhatdan WDYT? Should we scrap this or is there use in it?
Lets scrap it. Because user can just reset, and then edit storage.conf to do the changes you want correct?
Yeah you can just do the changes by hand, I will close this
